title: WELD: The Heirloom
tagline: A digital heirloom sealed for the heir at the tip of an unforgeable signed bloodline — no server, no lawyer, no trust.
holy_shit: Seal a letter, a key, or a memory with AES-256-GCM and bind it to a family line of ECDSA signatures: each generation signs the next's public key, the heir at the tip is the only one whose passphrase opens the seal — publish the sealed package to the whole world and it stays perfectly secret until the rightful heir arrives.
powered_by: The Artifact With a Signed Bloodline's ECDSA P-256 generational chain (each generation signs the next's public key) welded to Broadcast the Secret's AES-256-GCM (RSL1) seal — the heir at the tip of the verified bloodline is the one whose passphrase opens the sealed contents.
weld: Transplant the ECDSA generational chain from the-artifact-with-a-signed-bloodline (each generation's key signs the next's public key) and the AES-256-GCM RSL1 seal from broadcast-the-secret; weld point = the heir at the tip of the verified bloodline is the one whose passphrase opens the seal.
agent_scenario: When data must survive generations with verifiable chain-of-custody yet remain readable only by the current rightful holder: sign each new keyholder's public key with the prior generation's ECDSA key, seal the payload AES-256-GCM under the heir's passphrase, and publish both openly — anyone verifies the lineage, only the heir reads the contents.
author: kody-w + Copilot
